description: Letter from Ya’aqov b. Nahum b. Hakhmon, from Tripoli (Libya), to Nahray b. Nissim, Alexandria. Around 1060. The letter contains details about goods that arrived from Fustat. It is hard to unload them because there are goods on top of them. The writer mentions the prices of several goods and selling goods that Nahray sent to Tripoli. He asks Nahray to send the flax he bought for him through Sicily if there is no ship that sails from Alexandria to Tripoli. (Information from Gil, Kingdom, Vol. 4, #691) VMR
description_he: מכתב מיעקב בן נחום בן חכמון, מטריפולי, לנהראי בן ניסים, אלכסנדריה, כ-1060, על פריקת סחורות ומחירי סחורות שנשלחו לטריפולי
